sections.paragraph: m., -Jon. 4:610 ; Jerome, Syr., and others, ricinus; Palma Christi, Arab. الخروع Ægypt. κίκι, κούκι, (Diod. Sic. i. 34), a tall biennial plant, still cultivated in our gardens, beautiful and quick growing, with a soft and succulent stalk, a slight injury of which will cause the plant to die. LXX. cucumber; but see Bochart, Hieroz. t. ii. p. 293, 623. Celsii Hierob. P. ii. p. 273-82. Faber on Harmer’s Observations, vol. i. p. 140-151.
